allowed_values: ["Extensive inline comments", "Sparse inline comments", "Self-documenting code preference", "Mixed approach", "Docstring/documentation blocks only", "No comments"]
description: Preference for inline comments, documentation blocks, or minimal commenting in code.

allowed_values: ["Always includes file-level TLDR", "Always includes function-level TLDR", "Includes TLDR for complex functions only", "Rarely includes TLDR", "Never includes TLDR"]
description: Whether developer includes a summary, TLDR, or executive overview at the beginning of files or functions.

allowed_values: ["Highly verbose (long descriptive names)", "Moderately verbose", "Mixed (context-dependent)", "Terse abbreviated names", "Single-letter names"]
description: Style of naming variables, functions, and classes - verbose descriptive vs. terse abbreviated.
Preference for indentation width, tabs vs spaces, and whitespace formatting.
Preference for functional, object-oriented, procedural, or imperative programming paradigm.

allowed_values: ["Very small functions (5-10 lines)", "Small functions (10-30 lines)", "Medium functions (30-100 lines)", "Large functions (100+ lines)", "Varies by context"]
description: Preference for small focused functions vs larger multi-purpose functions.

allowed_values: ["Defensive with try/catch everywhere", "Explicit error returns/validation", "Graceful degradation and recovery", "Minimal error handling", "Silent failure tolerance"]
description: Approach to error handling - try/catch blocks, explicit returns, graceful degradation, or silent failures.

allowed_values: ["Highly abstract and generic", "Moderate abstraction", "Concrete problem-specific solutions", "Mixed approach", "No premature abstraction"]
description: Tendency to create reusable abstractions and generic solutions vs solving specific problems directly.

allowed_values: ["Lean on libraries (minimal custom code)", "Balance of libraries and custom", "Prefer custom implementations", "Use framework defaults", "Dependency minimalist"]
description: Preference for using libraries and frameworks vs writing custom implementations.

allowed_values: ["Performance-first optimization", "Readability-first then optimize if needed", "Balanced consideration", "Maintainability-first", "Premature optimization tendency"]
description: Whether developer prioritizes code performance optimization or code readability and maintainability.

allowed_values: ["Test-driven development (TDD)", "Comprehensive unit test coverage", "Mix of unit and integration tests", "Minimal testing", "No tests"]
description: Stance on unit tests, integration tests, TDD, and test coverage requirements.

allowed_values: ["Continuous refactoring (perfectionist)", "Regular refactoring cycles", "Refactor when necessary", "Rarely refactors", "Never refactors"]
description: How often code is refactored, cleaned up, and how much tolerance for technical debt.
